nodes.label: Are the NICE guideline versions needed for the Kumulus case set obtainable?

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.text: Whether the NICE guideline versions actually needed for the Kumulus case set are obtainable, given the NICE API's six-month trial appears to expose only current versions and the Kumulus cases may reference older ones

nodes.label: Is the NICE link on the non-Kumulus case a genuine clinical citation or generic appendix content?

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.text: Whether the NICE guideline link found in the clinical summary reflects the clinician actively citing the guideline (clinically applicable) or is just part of a generic appendix that happened to reference NICE content

nodes.label: Why did the NICE guideline link not load for Rowan?

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.text: Why the NICE guideline link did not load when Rowan clicked it

nodes.label: Identify NICE guideline versions referenced in Kumulus cases

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.description: Go into MedBrief, find the specific cases supplied to Kumulus, and check their clinical summaries, chronologies, and ECAs to identify which NICE guideline version(s) are actually referenced or applicable per case.
nodes.source_quote: 1. Go into MedBrief, find the specific cases supplied to Kumulus, and check their clinical summaries, chronologies, and ECAs to identify which NICE guideline version(s) are actually referenced or applicable per case.

nodes.label: Check NICE API availability for the identified guideline versions

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.description: Check whether those specific NICE guideline versions are available via the NICE API.
nodes.source_quote: 2. Check whether those specific versions are available via the NICE API.

nodes.label: Investigate alternative extraction route and store guidelines locally as a POC

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.description: If not available via the API: investigate scraping or another extraction route, then store the guidelines locally as a proof of concept.
nodes.source_quote: 3. If not available via the API: investigate scraping or another extraction route, then store the guidelines locally as a proof of concept.

nodes.label: Design longer-term strategy to keep guidelines current (secondary priority)

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.description: Explicitly secondary: design a longer-term strategy, such as a cron job, to keep guidelines current. Proving technical feasibility of extraction comes first.
nodes.source_quote: 4. Explicitly secondary: a longer-term strategy such as a cron job to keep guidelines current. Proving technical feasibility of extraction comes first.

nodes.label: Guidelines must not be stored on the MedBrief server

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: NICE guidelines should not be stored on the MedBrief server. They need a separate store, likely aligned with the broader data strategy (Data Lake, Databricks, or SQL Server mentioned as candidates, unspecified which). Not needed for the POC itself, only for eventual production.

nodes.label: NICE guideline licensing needs consideration

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: NICE guideline terms and conditions and licensing need consideration before guidelines can be extracted or stored; this was not resolved on the call.

nodes.label: Laura: NICE link may be a genuine clinical citation

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: The NICE guideline link found in the clinical summary might reflect the clinician actively quoting or citing the guideline, making it clinically applicable.

nodes.label: Deon: NICE link may just be generic appendix content

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: The NICE guideline link found in the clinical summary might just be part of a generic appendix that happened to reference NICE content, not a clinician's active citation.

nodes.label: Suspected cause for the NICE link failing to load

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: The NICE guideline link may have failed to load because Rowan was outside the UK, or because he was not logged into the NICE system; this cause is suspected but unconfirmed.

nodes.label: Approved Rowan's blob storage for AI-2066 testing

[narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: Approved the blob storage Rowan created under the MedBrief Secure Review subscription for AI-2066 translation testing, rather than any storage Laura may have had attached from her own prior testing.

nodes.label: Approved uploading the real PII-bearing German record

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: Approved uploading the real PII-bearing German record for translation testing.

nodes.label: Agreed to benchmark clinicalrecord-translator against embedded ground truth

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: Agreed to benchmark clinicalrecord-translator by extracting the German pages from the real sample document, translating them, and comparing the output against the English pages already embedded in the same document as ground truth. Proposed, not yet executed.

nodes.label: Agreed to benchmark other translation services before moving to real PHI

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.statement: Agreed next step: benchmark other translation services (AWS, Google, DeepL) on synthetic documents first; only move to real PHI through whichever service shows better results once it does.

nodes.label: Benchmark clinicalrecord-translator against embedded ground truth

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.description: Extract the German pages from the real sample document, run them through clinicalrecord-translator, and compare the output against the English pages already embedded in the same document as ground truth.
nodes.source_quote: Proposed benchmark idea, agreed but not yet done: extract the German pages from the real sample, run them through the translator, and compare against the English pages already embedded in the same document as ground truth.

nodes.label: Benchmark other translation services on synthetic documents

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.description: Benchmark other translation services (AWS, Google, DeepL) on synthetic documents first; only move to real PHI through whichever service shows better results.
nodes.source_quote: Agreed next step: benchmark other services (AWS, Google, DeepL) on synthetic documents first; only move to real PHI through them once one shows better results.

nodes.label: Is it okay to upload the real PII-bearing German record?

provenance.quote: [narration: paraphrased recollection, not a verbatim quote]

nodes.text: Whether uploading the real PII-bearing German record was okay
nodes.answer_summary: Yes; both Deon and Laura approved uploading it.

nodes.statement: Pseudonymisation testing has not been working well.

nodes.label: NICE guidelines for the Lexvision POC Kumulus project
nodes.summary: Laura asked Rowan to check which NICE guideline versions the Kumulus case set actually needs, whether they're reachable via the new (current-only) NICE API trial, and if not, to look into scraping and local storage as a proof of concept. Deon ruled out storing guidelines on the MedBrief server. An unresolved incident: Laura found a NICE guideline link in an unrelated case's clinical summary, of disputed relevance, that Rowan couldn't open.

nodes.label: AI-2066 translation project update
nodes.summary: Rowan showed Deon and Laura his own blob storage and the digital-path demo translation defects (untranslated banner, dropped diagnosis sentence, lost signature line breaks). New context surfaced: the real PHI sample is a transposed German/English document, suggesting a built-in benchmark. Agreed next step: test other translation services on synthetic data before touching real PHI.
